SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.examples

Class InletSeparatorSafetySystemExample

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.examples.InletSeparatorSafetySystemExample
public class InletSeparatorSafetySystemExample extends Object
Inlet Separator Safety-System Demonstration.

This example builds a small but realistic inlet-separation package for an offshore production manifold and overlays the full set of safety devices that would appear on its P&ID:

  • ESDV-1001 — emergency shutdown isolation valve on the inlet line (fail-closed, SIL-rated final element).
  • V-100 — three-phase inlet separator (design pressure 100 bara, MAWP 110 bara).
  • PT-1001 / TT-1001 — pressure and temperature transmitters on the gas outlet with HI/HIHI and LO/LOLO alarm configuration.
  • LT-1001 / LT-1002 — oil and water level transmitters with HI/HIHI and LO/LOLO alarms.
  • PSV-1001 — conventional spring-loaded relief valve protecting V-100; sized per API 520 for the controlling case (blocked gas outlet and external fire).
  • SIS — three Safety Instrumented Functions (PSH trip, LSL trip, fire-zone ESD) built using SafetyInstrumentedFunction with IEC 61511 SIL/PFD validation.
  • LOPA — Layer of Protection Analysis for the "Inlet Separator Overpressure" scenario showing how BPCS, PSH alarm, SIS trip and PSV combine to meet the target mitigated frequency.

The example is deliberately self-contained — running main(String[]) prints a complete safety report (instrument alarms, PSV orifice areas, SIS SIL/PFD/RRF, and LOPA worksheet) to the configured Log4j2 logger. The helper builders are exposed as package-visible methods so a unit test can assert against the structured results.

Standards referenced: API 520/521 (relief sizing), IEC 61508 / IEC 61511 (functional safety), NORSOK S-001 (technical safety), ISA 84.00.01 (SIS), CCPS Layer of Protection Analysis (LOPA).

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

  • Constructor Details

    • InletSeparatorSafetySystemExample

      public InletSeparatorSafetySystemExample()

  • Method Details

    • buildFeedFluid

      public SystemInterface buildFeedFluid()
      Builds the feed fluid used for the inlet separator example.

      Composition is a typical light NCS associated-gas / condensate / produced-water blend.

      Returns:
      a fully configured SystemInterface

    • buildProcess

      public ProcessSystem buildProcess()
      Builds the steady-state flowsheet: feed → ESDV-1001 → V-100 → gas / oil / water outlet valves.

      The PSV-1001 is configured separately in configurePressureSafetyValve() on a relief stream so it does not carry normal-operation flow, which mirrors the physical reality of a normally-closed spring-loaded PSV.

      Returns:
      the configured ProcessSystem

    • addInstrumentation

      public void addInstrumentation()
      Adds the inlet-separator instrumentation (PT, TT, LT pair) and configures HI/HIHI and LO/LOLO alarm thresholds.

      Alarm philosophy applied:

      • PT-1001: LO 50 bara, LOLO 35 bara, HI 85 bara, HIHI 95 bara (95% of design = 95 bara).
      • TT-1001: HI 80 °C, HIHI 90 °C (protect downstream coolers / hydrate margin).
      • LT-1001 oil: LO 30%, LOLO 15% (anti gas blow-by), HI 70%, HIHI 85% (carry-over).
      • LT-1002 water: HI 50%, HIHI 70% (interface upset).

    • configurePressureSafetyValve

      public SafetyValve configurePressureSafetyValve()
      Configures PSV-1001 and sizes it against two relieving scenarios:
      • Blocked gas outlet — full feed gas mass flow vented through the PSV (gas service, 10% overpressure per API 520).
      • External fire — reduced rate fire-case vapour generation (fire service, 21% overpressure).

      The PSV is configured outside the main ProcessSystem so steady-state mass balance is not perturbed by a normally-closed device. Sizing uses SafetyValveMechanicalDesign.calcDesign() which iterates over all registered SafetyValve.RelievingScenarios and stores the controlling orifice area.

      Returns:
      the configured SafetyValve

    • configureSafetyInstrumentedSystem

      public Map<String,SafetyInstrumentedFunction> configureSafetyInstrumentedSystem()
      Builds the three Safety Instrumented Functions that protect the inlet separator package.
      • SIF-001 Inlet ESD high-pressure trip — PT-1001 HIHI → close ESDV-1001 (SIL 2, ESD category).
      • SIF-002 Low-level gas-blow-by trip — LT-1001 LOLO → close LV-1001 (SIL 1, PSD category).
      • SIF-003 Fire & gas zonal ESD — confirmed fire/gas detection → close ESDV-1001 (SIL 2, F&G category).
      Returns:
      immutable map of SIF tag to SafetyInstrumentedFunction

    • buildLopaForOverpressure

      public LOPAResult buildLopaForOverpressure()
      Builds the LOPA worksheet for the "Inlet separator overpressure" consequence and demonstrates how Independent Protection Layers (IPLs) reduce the unmitigated event frequency to the target value.

      The protection layers credited (each independent and audited):

      • BPCS PC-1001 pressure control loop — PFD 1.0e-1 (IEC 61511 default for non-SIL BPCS).
      • PSH alarm + operator response — PFD 1.0e-1 (10 min response, audible/visual).
      • SIF-001 inlet ESD high-pressure trip — PFD 5.0e-3 (SIL 2).
      • PSV-1001 mechanical relief — PFD 1.0e-2 (well-maintained, proof-tested annually).

      Target frequency derived from STS 0131 overpressure category for an event pressure equal to MAWP and a typical tolerable risk frequency of 1.0e-4 /yr.

      Returns:
      populated LOPAResult

    • demonstrateOverpressureTrip

      public String demonstrateOverpressureTrip()
      Demonstrates an inlet-overpressure trip sequence: an upset drives PT-1001 above its HIHI alarm; the SIS executes SIF-001 which de-energises ESDV-1001; the valve closes within its stroke time.
      Returns:
      a short textual description of the trip outcome

    • runFullDemonstration

      public InletSeparatorSafetySystemExample.SafetyReport runFullDemonstration()
      Runs the full demonstration: build flowsheet, run steady-state, size PSV, build SIS and LOPA, then print the complete safety report. Suitable as the entry point of the example.
      Returns:
      a InletSeparatorSafetySystemExample.SafetyReport aggregating all results for downstream assertions

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      Convenience entry point.
      Parameters:
      args - command line arguments (ignored)

    • buildReliefStream

      private StreamInterface buildReliefStream(StreamInterface source, double pressureBara, double temperatureC, double flowKgHr)
      Builds a relief-scenario stream by cloning the protected stream and overriding pressure, temperature and flow. The clone is flashed at the relieving conditions so the sizing strategy can read Z, MW, gamma and density.
      Parameters:
      source - the protected stream
      pressureBara - relieving pressure in bara
      temperatureC - relieving temperature in deg C
      flowKgHr - relieving mass flow in kg/hr
      Returns:
      a fully initialized StreamInterface

    • logReport

      private void logReport(InletSeparatorSafetySystemExample.SafetyReport report)
      Emits a human-readable summary of the safety report to the logger.
      Parameters:
      report - the report to log

    • getProcess

      public ProcessSystem getProcess()
      Returns:
      the underlying ProcessSystem, or null until buildProcess() has been called

    • getInletEsdValve

      public ESDValve getInletEsdValve()
      Returns:
      the inlet ESD valve

    • getInletSeparator

      public ThreePhaseSeparator getInletSeparator()
      Returns:
      the three-phase inlet separator

    • getReliefValve

      public SafetyValve getReliefValve()
      Returns:
      the relief valve (only populated after configurePressureSafetyValve())

    • getSifs

      public Map<String,SafetyInstrumentedFunction> getSifs()
      Returns:
      the SIS map (only populated after configureSafetyInstrumentedSystem())
